November Fifteenth, Two Thousand Twenty Six

November Fifteenth, Two Thousand Twenty Six
Ten in the Morning
Dinner and Dancing to Follow
our story

We met during junior year at Michigan — both reaching for the same dog-eared Rushdie novel in Hatcher Library. What started as study sessions turned into late-night walks, turned into chai at midnight, turned into years of showing up for each other across cities and time zones. On the rooftop of a crumbling haveli at sunset in Rajasthan, Arjun asked. She said yes before he finished the sentence.

“Then they both cried. Then they ordered samosas.”

While You're Here

City Palace

A sprawling complex of courtyards and terraces overlooking Lake Pichola. Allow at least two hours and wear comfortable shoes.

Lake Pichola

Take a sunset boat ride past the Lake Palace and Jag Mandir. The light on the water in November is extraordinary.

Jagdish Temple

Seventeenth-century carved marble in the heart of the old city. Early morning visits are the quietest.

Ambrai Ghat

The best sunset spot in Udaipur. Arrive by five for a table at the water's edge.

Thank you from the bottom of our hearts for celebrating with us
